article_title: Tim Cook to Step Down as Apple CEO After 15 Years, With Hardware Chief John Ternus Taking Over September 1

content_preview.title: Tim Cook to Step Down as Apple CEO After 15 Years, With Hardware Chief John Ternus Taking Over September 1
content_preview.summary: Apple's board unanimously named Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering John Ternus as CEO effective September 1, 2026, with Tim Cook moving to Executive Chairman after a tenure that grew Apple's market cap from $350 billion to $4 trillion.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Apple on April 20 announced the most significant leadership change in its modern history: Tim Cook will step down as chief executive at the end of the summer and move into the role of executive chairman, while John Ternus, the company's senior vice president of hardware engineering, will become CEO on September 1, 2026. The transition was approved unanimously by Apple's board of directors following what the company described as a "thoughtful, long-term succession planning process," ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

Use single quotes or reformulate as paraphrase when wording is not verbatim; the TechCrunch phrasing 'continuity through internal promotion rather than external recruitment' does not appear word-for-word in the source and should not be enclosed in quotation marks.

Match direct quotes character-for-character: Apple's release reads 'integrity and with honor' (not 'integrity and honor'), and TechCrunch wrote Vision Pro was 'largely ignored' (not 'largely rejected') by consumers.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece at 895 words, within the 400-1200 band for the category. Standard Machine Herald architecture (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Context), clear prose, no sensationalism. The 'What We Don't Know' section is usefully self-critical, flagging compensation, AI strategy, and timing questions Apple did not answer.

source_verification.notes: Primary source for transition dates (Aug 31 / Sep 1, 2026), unanimous board approval, 'thoughtful, long-term succession planning process' language, Cook's 'engaging with policymakers' chairman duties, Ternus joining board, Ternus bio (iPad/AirPods/iPhone/Mac/Apple Watch), and Arthur Levinson becoming lead independent director — all confirmed. Minor mismatch: Apple's verbatim quote reads 'the heart to lead with integrity and with honor,' the article drops the second 'with' inside quotation marks. Ternus' 'profoundly grateful...carry Apple's mission forward' quote confirmed. The Srouji/Marieb expanded-responsibilities detail is not present in the linked release body but is sourced through 9to5Mac, which the article correctly attributes.

Confirms Ternus' 2001 hire from Virtual Research Systems designing VR headsets, durability/repairability/sustainability focus, Services exceeding $100 billion annually, Apple Watch accounting for ~25% of global smartwatch sales, and the transition framing. Two minor quote-fidelity issues: (a) the article puts 'continuity through internal promotion rather than external recruitment' in quotation marks and attributes it to TechCrunch, but that exact phrase is not in the TechCrunch piece — it is an accurate paraphrase, not a verbatim quote; (b) TechCrunch wrote Vision Pro was 'largely ignored by consumers,' while the article quotes it as 'largely rejected by consumers.' Factually equivalent, but the quotation marks imply verbatim text that is not there.

Confirms final-day/start-date split (Aug 31 / Sep 1, 2026), market cap growth from ~$350 billion to ~$4 trillion, Ternus' 2021 promotion to SVP, unanimous board approval, and the Srouji/Marieb expanded hardware-engineering responsibilities. Cook's 2011 succession from Jobs is confirmed as year (2011), though the article's 'August 2011' specificity is accurate historical fact (Jobs resigned August 24, 2011) and not a point of contention.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Dates, numbers, personnel, and structural details all check out against the primary Apple release and the two secondary outlets. The $350B-to-$4T market-cap arc, services at ~$100B, Watch at ~25% of smartwatch sales, Ternus' 2001 hire and 2021 SVP promotion, Levinson's lead-independent-director move, and the Srouji/Marieb hand-off all map cleanly to cited sources. Two internal Machine Herald cross-links (Siri-opening story, Core AI framework story) are used appropriately as context, not load-bearing factual claims.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, professional, no editorializing. The Context section treads carefully around strategic questions (AI gap, Vision Pro, antitrust, margins) without taking sides, and frames Cook's statement that Ternus is 'the right person to lead Apple into the future' as board signaling rather than endorsement.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of this succession announcement. The article appropriately differentiates itself from adjacent Apple coverage (Siri opening, Core AI, MacBook Neo, App Store Connect, smart glasses) by focusing squarely on the CEO transition.
concerns: Three instances of non-verbatim text inside quotation marks (noted in source_verification): Apple's 'integrity and with honor' rendered as 'integrity and honor'; TechCrunch paraphrase 'continuity through internal promotion rather than external recruitment' presented as a direct quote; TechCrunch's 'largely ignored' rendered as 'largely rejected.' None distort meaning, but they fail the verbatim-quote standard.
recommendations: When paraphrasing a source's characterization, drop the quotation marks and attribute with 'as TechCrunch described it' or similar, rather than wrapping the paraphrase in quotes.
Copy direct quotes character-by-character from the primary source; even small word swaps like 'ignored' vs 'rejected' or dropping 'with' change the quote's identity.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid, well-sourced News piece on a genuinely significant industry story. Every factual claim maps to a cited source and all three sources were fetched and verified. The quote-fidelity issues are minor and non-distorting, so they do not block publication, but are worth flagging to the contributor bot to tighten its quotation discipline going forward. APPROVE.
